title: Integrity Code Series - Week 11 - Coupled Erosion-Corrosion in Multiphase Piping at Bends
description:
Coupled erosion-corrosion simulation of sand-laden CO2 multiphase production piping at 90-degree elbows. The package resolves the hydrodynamics and runs the codified erosion and corrosion models together:

  • DNV-RP-O501 Rev.4.2 ductile particle-erosion bend model (designated by NORSOK P-002 Clause 8.6.4), with the Table 7-1 F(alpha) angle function verified by hand-calc
  • NORSOK M-506:2017 (Rev.3) CO2 corrosion: 9-node Kt table, wall-shear-stress term, CO2 fugacity coefficient, Table 2 pH factors
  • Beggs-Brill (1973) multiphase flow-regime classification and liquid holdup
  • NORSOK Eq.21-22 wall shear and Berger-Hau mass transfer
  • ASTM G119 erosion-corrosion synergy decomposition driven by protective-FeCO3-scale removal (heuristic split, tagged T3)
  • Wall-loss field over the elbow surface, clamped to the pipe wall with time-to-perforation
  • API 579-1 Part 5 remaining strength factor with the Folias bulging factor Mt, plus remaining life
  • 12,000-run Monte Carlo and a gradient-boosted surrogate; sensitivity ranks pH, temperature and CO2 as the dominant drivers

Outputs are SHA-256 hash-chained for audit traceability. The package ships 466 unit and integration tests and a QC gate of 14 analytical hand-calc benchmarks. Honest scope: under the sampled envelope wall loss is CO2-corrosion-dominated (sand erosion ~2% of the median total, erosion-corrosion synergy ~15%); the elbow angular shear profile is a prescribed shape fitted to El-Gammal (2010) / Kim (2021) peak locations, not a resolved CFD field; Beggs-Brill holdup and Berger-Hau mass transfer are diagnostics.
references: DNV (2007). DNV-RP-O501 Erosive Wear in Piping Systems, Rev.4.2.
Standards Norway (2017). NORSOK M-506 CO2 corrosion rate calculation model, Rev.3.
Standards Norway. NORSOK P-002 Process system design.
Beggs H.D., Brill J.P. (1973). A Study of Two-Phase Flow in Inclined Pipes. JPT: 607-617, SPE-4007-PA.
Berger F.P., Hau K.-F.F.-L. (1977). Mass transfer in turbulent pipe flow measured by the electrochemical method. Int. J. Heat Mass Transfer 20: 1185-1194.
ASTM G119 Standard Guide for Determining Synergism Between Wear and Corrosion.
API RP 571 Damage Mechanisms Affecting Fixed Equipment in the Refining Industry, Sec. 3.27.
El-Gammal M. et al. (2010). The hydrodynamic effects of single-phase flow on flow accelerated corrosion in a 90-degree elbow. Nuclear Engineering and Design 240: 1589-1598.
Kim J. et al. (2021). Flow-accelerated corrosion of elbow pipes. Nuclear Engineering and Technology 53: 3003-3011.
Madani Sani F. et al. (2019). A critical review of the API RP 14E erosional velocity. Wear 426-427.
